lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're a wise old man, huh?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Less of the 'old' man, if you please!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But yes, I suppose you could say that. I prefer to think of myself as a sage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's a sage doing here?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've spent most of my life studying this world in which we live. I've strode through the depths of the deadliest dungeons, roamed the murky jungles of Karamja, meditated on the glories of Saradomin on Entrana,

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: and read some dusty tomes in the Library of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now I'm not as young as I used to be, I'm settling here where it's peaceful.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It's a pity about that vampyre that keeps attacking the village. At least Saradomin protects me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's quite an exciting life you've had.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Exciting? Yes, I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now I'm here, perhaps I could offer you the benefit of my experience and wisdom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! So you can help me?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, I imagine you've gathered up quite a lot of stuff on your travels. Things you used for quests a long time ago that you don't need now.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you like, I can look through your bank and see if there's anything you can chuck away.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Alternatively, you can bring items here and show them to me. If I see that it's something you don't need, I'll let you know. I might even be willing to buy it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll help me clear junk out of my bank?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, that's right. Or I'd be happy to chat with you about the wonders of this world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have some free stuff, please?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Deary deary me...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm not giving out free money, but if you try again later I'd be glad to reward you if you'd do a little job for me.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm not giving out free money, but I'd be happy to reward you if you'll do a little job for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, what do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm sure I can think of a few little jobs. This won't be a quest, mind you, just a little favour...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd just like to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm sure I can think of a few little jobs. This won't be a quest, mind you, just a little favour...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you check my items for junk, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I get another hood for my Quest Point Cape?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You don't appear to have enough space in your inventory. Come back and see me when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a Quest Point Cape, please?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Gladly, though they cost 99,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, 99,000 coins is fine.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: All skillcapes come with a hood - you're not going to be able to hold them now. Try again another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have to come back another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thanks, but I don't have anything I need doing right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you check my items for junk, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd just like to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me about the Wilderness, please?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If Entrana is a land dedicated to the glory of Saradomin, the Wilderness is surely the land of Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It's a dangerous place, where adventurers such as yourself may attack each other, using all their combat skills in the struggle for survival.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The Wilderness has different levels. In a low level area, you can only fight adventurers whose combat level is close to yours.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But if you venture into the high level areas in the far north, you can be attacked by adventurers who are significantly stronger than you.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Of course, you'd be able to attack considerably weaker people too, so it can be worth the risk.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you dare to go to the far north-west of the Wilderness, there's a building called the Mage Arena where you can learn to summon the power of Saradomin himself!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: As you wish. Farewell, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about jungles?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If it's jungle you want, look no further than the southern regions of Karamja.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Once you get south of Brimhaven, the whole island is pretty much covered in exotic trees, creepers and shrubs.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a small settlement called Tai Bwo Wannai Village in the middle of the island. It's a funny place; the chieftain's an unfriendly chap and his sons are barking mad.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Honestly, one of them asked me to stuff a dead monkey with seaweed so he could EAT it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've met them.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Further south you'll find Shilo Village. It's been under attack by terrifying zombies in recent months, if my sources are correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've dealt with them, although there are a few still knocking about.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The jungle's filled with nasty creatures. There are vicious spiders that you can hardly see before they try to bite your legs off, and great big jungle ogres.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about what's underground.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh, the dwarven realms?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, there was a time, back in the Fourth Age, when we humans wouldn't have been able to venture underground. That was before we had magic; the dwarves were quite a threat.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Still, it's much more friendly now. You can visit the vast dwarven mine if you like; the entrance is on the mountain north of Falador.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you go further west you may be able to visit the dwarven city of Keldagrim. But they were a bit cautious about letting humans in, last time I asked.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: On the other hand, if you go west of Brimhaven, you'll find a huge underground labyrinth full of giants, demons, dogs and dragons to fight. It's even bigger than the caves under Taverley, although the Taverley

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: dungeon's pretty good for training your combat skills.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What mystical realms can I visit?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, you've been to Zanaris. I met a fairy years ago who said she lived there. She was an incredible warrior, considering that she wasn't even a metre tall.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Also, in my research I came across ancient references to some kind of Abyss. Demons from the Abyss have already escaped into this land; Saradomin be thanked that they are very rare!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been through a portal into the lair of some ancient demon. It was in the city of Uzer.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah, Uzer. A city much favoured by Saradomin, destroyed by Thammaron, the elder-demon, late in the Third Age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, there isn't much left there.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the biggest monster in the world?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a mighty fire-breathing dragon living underground in the deep Wilderness, known as the King Black Dragon. It's a fearsome beast, with a breath that can poison you, freeze you to the ground or

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: incinerate you where you stand.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But even more deadly is the Queen of the Kalphites. As if her giant mandibles of death were not enough, she also throws her spines at her foes with deadly force. She can even cast rudimentary spells.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Some dark power must be protecting her, for she can block attacks using prayer just as humans do.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Another beast that's worthy of a special mention is the Shaikahan. It dwells in the eastern reaches of Karamja, and is almost impossible to kill except with specially prepared weapons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, that one's dead. I've been helping a barmy hunter who'd sworn to kill it.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does poison do?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Many monsters use poison against their foes. If you get poisoned, you will not feel it at the time, but later you will begin to suffer its effects, and your life will drain slowly from you.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Over time the effects dwindle to nothing, but if you had already been wounded you might die before they wear off completely.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Fortunately, followers of Guthix have devised potions that can cure the poison or even give immunity to it.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Should you wish to use poison against your own enemies and those of our Lord Saradomin, there is a potion that you can smear on your daggers, arrows, spears, javelins and throwing knives.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What monsters drop good items?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: As a general rule, tougher monsters drop more valuable items. But even a lowly hobgoblin can drop valuable gems; it just does this extremely rarely.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you can persuade the Slayer Masters to train you as a Slayer, you will be able to fight certain monsters that drop valuable items far more often.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You might care to invest in an enchanted dragonstone ring. These are said to make a monster drop its most valuable items a little more often.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are these strange monsters that keep appearing out of nowhere and attacking me when I'm training?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah, I imagine you see a lot of those.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Creatures such as the rock golem, river troll and tree spirit dwell in places where adventurers frequently go to train their skills. While you're training you will often disturb one by accident. It will then get angry and

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: attack you. The safest way to deal with them is to run away immediately, but they sometimes drop valuable items if you kill them.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about valiant heroes!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ha ha ha... There are plenty of heroes. Always have been, always will be, until the fall of the world.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you'd do a few more quests, you'd soon become a fairly noted adventurer yourself.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You're quite a noted adventurer yourself!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But I suppose I could tell you of a couple...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, there was a man called Arrav. No-one knew where he came from, but he was a fearsome fighter, a skillful hunter and a remarkable farmer. He lived in the ancient settlement of Avarrocka, defending it from

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: goblins, until he went forth in search of some strange artefact long desired by the dreaded Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Perhaps some day I shall be able to tell you what became of him.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But do not let your head be turned by heroics. Randas was another great man, but he let himself be beguiled into turning to serve Zamorak, and they say he is now a mindless creature deep in the Underground Pass that

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: leads to Isafdar.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did humans learn to use magic?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah, that was quite a discovery! It revolutionised our way of life and jolted us into this Fifth Age of the world.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: They say a traveller in the north discovered the key, although no records state exactly what he found. From this he was able to summon the magic of the four elements, using magic as a tool and a weapon. He and

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: his followers learnt how to bind the power into little stones so that others could use it.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: in the land south of here they constructed an immense tower where the power could be studied, but followers of Zamorak destroyed it with fire many years ago, and most of the knowledge was lost.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Perhaps one day those lost secrets will be uncovered once more.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I hear you've been very helpful to the wizards who are working on the mysteries of the rune stones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I didn't do much...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you'd know about the history of today's cities?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, there are fairly good records of the formation of the cities from primitive settlements.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: In the early part of the Fourth Age, of course, there were no permanent settlements. Tribes wandered the lands, staying where they could until the resources were exhausted.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: This changed as people learnt to grow crops and breed animals, and now there are very few of the old nomadic tribes. There's at least one tribe roaming between the Troll Stronghold and Rellekka, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've met them. They're all mad.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Truly, their faith in natural spirits is rather foolish, but they're a pleasant enough people.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Anyway, what was I saying? Ah, yes...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: One settlement was Avarrocka, a popular trading centre.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: In the west, Ardougne gradually formed under the leadership of the Carnillean family, despite the threat of the Mahjarrat warlord Hazeel who dwelt in that area until his downfall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He'll be back, just you wait!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh dear, I hope you haven't done anything rash!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've foiled a dastardly Zamorakian plot to have Hazeel resurrected.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So I'd heard. Lord Saradomin is smiling on you.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself, old man.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah, so you want to know about me, eh?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mmm... what could I say about myself? Let's see what I've done...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've delved into the dungeon west of Brimhaven and heard the terrifying CRASH of the steel dragons battling each other for territory.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I spent some years on Entrana, where I learnt the techniques of pure meditation.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've wandered through the vast desert that lies south of Al Kharid and seen the great walls of Menaphos and Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That feline statue you've got on your bookshelf there - I'm sure I saw something like that when I was in Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've always liked to collect little trinkets to remind me of the time when my life was more - ah - exciting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've spoken to the Sphinx, then?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Spoken to a sphinx, you say? My dear young lady, you can't possibly be serious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ha ha ha! The people who live deep in the shifting sands of the desert will often tell travellers tall tales of strange beasts, but that doesn't mean any of it's remotely true!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've met the Sph...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please, adventurer, there is nothing to be gained by interfering in matters you do not understand.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Even if such a creature were to exist...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... which it does...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: EVEN IF SUCH A CREATURE WERE TO EXIST it would be terribly dangerous for you to get involved in its business.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I have heard of terrible things happening to travellers in that land, and I would not wish for you to be dragged into anything like that.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Indeed, I have heard of adventurers doing the most shameful and blasphemous deeds believing themselves to be acting on behalf of some false god!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So please do not speak to me of this matter again. Now, I believe I was telling you of my adventurous youth...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Apart from all that, I've spent many a happy hour in dusty libraries, searching through ancient scrolls and texts for the wisdom of those who have passed on.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Plus plenty of other adventures, quests, journeys... Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard that Gielinor has three gods.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Indeed. This is correct: Saradomin, Guthix and Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Saradomin, the great and glorious, gives life to this world.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Zamorak craves only death and destruction.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Guthix, calling itself a god of 'balance', holds no allegiance, but simply aids whatever cause suits its shifting purpose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So who's Zaros?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: What did you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I discovered a buried altar to a god called Zaros.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please, do not speak further of this. No good can come of meddling in such things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Zaros is real?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Adventurer, my patience is limited. Speak to me no further of this - this - this foolishness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that mean I shouldn't ask you about the ancient magicks I learnt in that pyramid north of Sophanem?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ENOUGH! Begone from me, you foul trickster!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard of another god, called Armadyl.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: What is this of which you speak?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A strange man asked me to go into the Temple of Ikov and steal the Staff of Armadyl.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: And what makes you believe in this 'Armadyl'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there were servants of Armadyl living down in the depths of the temple. They'd been there for generations, guarding the staff.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So you trusted the word of these people who had been sitting down a hole all their lives?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Yes, I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I wouldn't worry about this sacred 'armadyllo' anymore. Just another foolish myth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well, the servants rewarded me very kindly for defeating Lucien for them.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: That is an impressive feat, adventurer. Truly you must be beloved in the eyes of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's lucky there's no Armadyl! I've pinched his staff and given it to Lucien.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: YOU'VE DONE WHAT?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Adventurer, I beg of you, do not meddle in the gods' affairs. No good can come of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about Tumeken, Elindis, Icthlarin and the Devourer?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: What names are these?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Down in Sophanem, they've got a chief god named Tumeken, plus a fertility goddess named Elindis. Icthlarin is their god of the dead and the Devourer...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Adventurer, what folly leads you to think any of these - things, exist?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've met Icthlarin! He'd got a jackal's head!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It is hot in that land, and the heat can affect a traveller in strange ways. Tell me, when you met 'Icthlarin', had you been feeling at all unwell?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, now you mention it, I'd just been hypnotised by a weird woman I met in the desert.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So a strange woman interfered with your mind, then you met a man with the head of a jackal, and you decided he must be a god?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, make sure you don't get hypnotised again, and I'm sure you'll not meet any more jackal-headed men claiming to be gods!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It'd probably be best if you don't go there again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you've been all around that area, haven't you? When you were travelling?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Indeed, I have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'd know all about their gods?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I know what I know.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: And knowing what I know, I do not speak of this matter. Perhaps you would be wise to heed my example.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanna know about the wars of the gods!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah, that was a terrible time. The armies of Saradomin fought gloriously against the minions of Zamorak, but many brave warriors and noble cities were overthrown and destroyed utterly.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You have visited Uzer, and seen for yourself the damage that could be wrought by an elder-demon in its wrath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did it all end?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Before the Zamorakian forces could be utterly routed, Lord Saradomin took pity on them and the battle- scarred world, and allowed a truce.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the Mahjarrat?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Very little is written about the tribe of the Mahjarrat. They are believed to be from the realm of Freneskae, or Frenaskrae - the spelling in this tongue is only approximate.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: One of them, the foul Zamorak, has achieved godhood, although none knows how this came about.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Other Mahjarrat who have been particularly active upon this plane are Hazeel, Lucien, Azzanadra and Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazeel will rise again, you'll see!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Adventurer, your actions fill me with concern!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've foiled a dastardly Zamorakian plot to have Hazeel resurrected.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So I'd heard. Lord Saradomin is smiling on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've defeated Lucien.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Truly, your bravery exceeds your stature. But I fear his defeat will not be permanent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met Lucien. He paid me very nicely for bringing him a staff he wanted.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Traveller, I beg of you, do not have further dealings with such creatures! You do not know their power as I do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Azzanadra taught me some ancient magical powers after I unlocked the pyramid where he had been trapped.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Leave me, traveller; I am old, and your words fill me with horror.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I wield the power of Saradomin myself?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I see you are already learning to summon the power of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you travel to the Mage Arena in the north-west reaches of the Wilderness, the battle mage Kolodion may be willing to let you learn to summon the power of Saradomin, should you be able to pass his test.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to ask you about your hat.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Why, thank you! I rather like it myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you steal it from the bank?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Me? Steal from the bank? Whatever do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's been a break-in at Draynor Bank, and I bet you did it.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh my goodness, what in the world could make you think I did such a thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you've been sitting here looking at the bank ever since you moved into this house. Now someone's robbed the bank, and suddenly your house is full of expensive stuff!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So what do you think I did? March across the street, smash the bank wall, knock out the bankers and steal all the money?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm an old man! I walk with a stick! How could I possibly have done that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't rob the bank?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Just run along now, be a good lad, and don't worry yourself about me or my finances!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... But I'll be keeping an eye on you.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now you've got that off your chest, would you like to ask me about anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You stole it off that woman in the bank! You stole all this valuable stuff too!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Stole it? How could you possibly think I did such a thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saw you robbing the bank! You killed all those people!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Deary me, [player name], your imagination is running wild! What could make you think you saw me do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen a security recording that shows you robbing the bank. The bank's guard showed it to me.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You've seen the bank's security recording?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Tut tut tut... Oh well, at least you'll never be able to tell the bank about me. They'll never listen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're just going to get away with it?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: That's my plan, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that's... well, it's WRONG!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Wrong? WRONG? I'll tell you what's wrong!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've spent my whole life travelling the world, doing quests for people, saving lives, saving villages from terrifying monsters and all that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now I'm old, and where do I have to live? In this freezing old house next to a pig-sty, with a bunch of yobs outside who can't keep their hands off the market stalls! What sort of reward is that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So don't talk to me about right and wrong!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe someone SHOULD talk to you about right and wrong...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Bah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmph!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now you've got that off your chest, would you like to ask me about anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I get a hat like that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You could buy one off another player, or wait until they're next made available by the Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy your hat?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ohhh no, I don't intend to part with this. Would you like to ask me about something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should give it back, you know.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, I think I'll keep it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now you've got that off your chest, would you like to ask me about anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was quite an exciting quest we did together!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh yes, it was certainly a fitting end to my adventuring days. Your fight against the Sea Troll Queen was highly impressive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you tempted to go on another quest in the future?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, I feel I am a bit old for that kind of thing now. New times need new heroes, and I'm sure you'll always be there to answer the call!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll certainly see what I can do!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When we finished fighting those sea trolls, you said you'd finished with adventuring, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, that's right. I'm an old man, there's no denying it, and I need a nice quiet retirement.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Besides, I think you've proved that the new generation has its own heroes and adventurers - I should step back and let you get on with it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does this mean you're not going to do any more big robberies?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, since you saved my life when we were fighting the Sea Troll Queen, I don't mind telling you... I trust you won't tell anyone about this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I might just have something in mind. After all, that little bank incident went very smoothly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're retired!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I retired from adventuring and questing. The episode in the bank was hardly a quest, more like a shopping trip!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your shopping trip killed 3 bank staff and several innocent bystanders, not to mention the watchman!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've saved countless villages from unspeakable terrors and slain innumerable evil monsters - surely I deserve some sort of reward in return? Yet what did I get? NOTHING!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You yourself would get pretty angry if you did a long and dangerous quest and didn't get any reward from it, eh? I'm sure you would!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well imagine how I felt at the end of a long life of questing when was left penniless, and I had to move into this thief-infested village! This little shack was falling apart when I arrived!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You go speak to that hag who keeps looking through my window - she'll show you how it used to look before I could afford to redecorate it!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Being noble and virtuous simply doesn't put food on the table, and I don't intend to sit in banks begging like so many people do these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So all those quests in the past, when you saved people from monsters and stuff like that... You only helped them because you wanted a reward?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, it wasn't always like that. I used to be satisfied just knowing that I'd helped, and that was reward enough.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But eventually I realised that people forgot about me as soon as the danger was gone, and I wasn't even getting paid properly for my services. Sometimes they offered training in the various skills, but that's no use to me!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I eventually realised that the only way I could get a proper reward for my life's efforts is if I took the reward myself. So I took up my cape and staff and headed for the bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And now you're planning to do another robbery? Didn't you get enough from the bank?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Perhaps not... But enough of this matter! Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

sections.title: Using items on Wise Old Man

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I suppose you could use that in combat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, old man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I suppose you could use that for defense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, old man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Runes are very useful things, but I'll take some of those off your hands for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ooh, runes? Yes, they're quite valuable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: That's called 'money'. Money is fantastically important if you haven't got enough of it, but when you've got as much as I have, you realise that money doesn't matter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

sections.title: Book on chickens, Book of folklore, or Strange book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: That's mine. I'll take it back, thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: How did you get your hands on that?!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Get out of here this instant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Tell you what, rather than you showing me items individually, I'll look through all your possessions to see if I can find anything worth discarding.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Humph! You could at least say hello before waving your items in my face.

sections.title: Searching through his bookcases without having talked to him first

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It'd be politer to come and talk to me before you start messing around with my possessions!

sections.title: If the Wise Old Man is in the Old man's coffin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, he's not here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see you've got your telescope pointing at the Wizards' Tower.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh, do I? Well, why does that interest you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It just seems a bit odd.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Odd? There's nothing odd about a fascination with the magical arts. Besides, the architecture of that Tower is truly remarkable, for one who studies such things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're not planning to attack the Tower?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, I wouldn't dream of doing anything of the sort!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you robbed a bank, and I bet you're now planning something to do with that Tower!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, no. I'm not planning anything like that again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll be watching you...

sections.title: Delivering items to the Wise Old Man

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, this is rather embarrassing, but I think there's some kind of monster in my house. Could you go upstairs and get rid of it, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I think it's somewhere upstairs. It kept me awake all last night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need some bronze bars. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mine some copper ore and tin ore. The furnace in Lumbridge can smelt them into bars of bronze.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need a few bronze daggers to keep the goblins at bay. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like some maces made of bronze. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need a few bronze arrowheads to complete some arrows I was making. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like some bronze knives to throw at goblins. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My weapons collection isn't all it used to be. Some short swords made of bronze would be much appreciated. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The head fell off my axe when I was chopping wood last week, so I need some more bronze axes. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Could you fetch me some big warhammers made of bronze? Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I could do with some medium-sized helmets. I'd like bronze ones. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need some lengths of bronze wire to repair something. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make [daggers/maces/arrowheads/throwing knives/short swords/axes/warhammers/helmets/wire].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need some iron bars. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mine some iron ore. The furnace in Lumbridge can smelt them into bars of iron.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need some iron arrowheads to put on the arrows I was making. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I could do with some iron throwing knives to keep the goblins away from my house. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like some chunky iron warhammers. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Some iron maces would be useful. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make [arrowtips/throwing knives/warhammers/maces].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: A few lumps of iron ore would be nice. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It should be easy enough for you to mine some iron ore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've found a use for cadava berries. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Just look around the woods south-west of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The hail can be very heavy here, so I'd like a few leather cowls. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to make the cowls yourself. Buy needles & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid if you need any.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Strange as it may seem, I want a lot of beer! Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a pub not far from here that will sell you plenty of that. Go west to Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I don't have a decent larder here, so I can't store food very well. Now I'm out of loaves of bread. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mix some flour and water to make bread dough, then bake it on a cooking range.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'll be needing a few pieces of soft leather. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a tanner in Al Kharid who will turn cowhides into leather.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I saw an interesting bed in Karamja called a 'hammock'. It seemed to be made out of string, and I'd like some balls of wool so I can make my own. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You can buy shears from the general store east of here. Shear some sheep, then spin the wool into balls on the spinning wheel in Lumbridge Castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It's a bit nippy in this house, and my hands are cold. I need some leather gloves. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to sew the gloves yourself. Buy a needle & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm going to make an omelette, so I'll need some eggs. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Eggs are usually found where chickens are farmed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My undergarments are getting a bit worn out. I'll be needing some sheets of silk to patch them. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a man in Al Kharid who sells it very cheaply. If you ever take any to Ardougne you'll get a good profit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'll need some clay that's been softened so I can craft it. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you mine some clay, you can use containers of water on it to soften it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Believe it or not, I need a few unfired clay pots. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mine some clay. Then use a container full of water to soften it. In the Barbarian Village you'll be able to form this into pots. Just don't bake them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My roof is leaking, so I need some swamp paste to fix it. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Swamp tar is found south-east of here. Add it to a pot of flour and cook it on an open fire to make the paste.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need some potatoes, if you'd be so kind. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a field of those north of Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I want to make some arrows, so I'll need some headless arrows that have the feathers attached. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Use a knife to chop some logs into arrowshafts. Then add a feather to each arrowshaft.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need some bronze spears. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you kill some of those pesky goblins, you're bound to get some bronze spears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My glassware got damaged when I moved here, so I need some new beer glasses. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you get some seaweed and a bucket of sand, you'll be able to mix them at a furnace to make molten glass. Then use a glassblowing pipe to make beer glasses. Most of what you need can be found on Entrana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My plans for today require some sets of bones, the normal-sized sort you get from goblins. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You'll find bones easily enough if you fight creatures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Strange as this may seem, I need some unfired pie dishes. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mine some clay. Then use a container full of water to soften it. In the Barbarian Village you'll be able to form this into pie dishes. Just don't bake them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I hear pet cats are getting popular these days. I'd like some raw rat in case I get one. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You should find some big rats south-east of here in the swamp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need a few lumps of copper ore. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It should be easy enough for you to mine some copper ore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm out of flour, so I could do with a few pots of that. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a windmill north of here. You can use it to grind grain into flour. Then use an empty pot to collect the flour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need a handful of feathers to stick in my beard. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The cheapest way to get feathers is to kill chickens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: This house is a bit cold, so I could do with some normal logs to burn. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I suggest you take an axe and chop down some standard trees.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm running short of food, so I'd like some cooked chickens. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You could try killing some chickens, then cooking their meat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My footwear is getting a bit worn out, so I need a few pairs of leather boots. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to make the boots yourself. Buy needles & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid if you need any.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like a few cowhides. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you slaughter some cows, you'll get cowhides easily enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: My shortbow's string is getting a bit worn out, so could you fetch me some bow strings? Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you pick some flax, you can use the spinning wheel in Lumbridge Castle to spin it into bowstrings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm a bit hungry. I'd like some cooked meat, not chicken or any sort of bird. And definitely not camel or rabbit either! Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Just cook some beef, rat or bear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm short of ammunition, so I could do with some bronze arrows. That way I can shoot at goblins through my window. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You can make them by chopping a log into arrowshafts and adding feathers to them, then smithing a bronze bar into arrowheads to complete the arrows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like a bit of grain. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There's a field full of it north of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need a few lumps of tin ore. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You shouldn't have any trouble mining tin ore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I could do with some freshly cooked [anchovies/shrimps] for a salad I'm planning. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Use a small fishing net to get [anchovies/shrimps] from the sea south of here. Then cook them on a fire or range.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like to study the rune essence that the wizards have been talking about recently, so I need a few pieces. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ask the Archmage in the tower south of here to teleport you to the Rune Essence mine. Then you can mine me some pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Some chunks of molten glass would be the ideal patch for my cracked window. Please bring me [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get that?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Use a bucket of sand with some soda ash on a furnace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've killed a creature that was under your bed.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah, thank you very much! Now I shall be able to sleep in peace.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Allow me to offer you an appropriate reward for your assistance...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some of the stuff you wanted.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ahh, you are very kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back when I've got the rest.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got all the stuff you asked me to fetch.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! In thanks, I shall bestow on you a simple blessing.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! Please take this money as a sign of my gratitude.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! Please take these seeds as a sign of my gratitude.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! Please take these herbs as a sign of my gratitude.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! Please take these runes as a sign of my gratitude.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! Please take this gem as a sign of my gratitude.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I've got the items you wanted.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Those are banknotes! I can't use those!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got all the stuff you asked me to fetch.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd give you a reward, but you don't seem to have any space for it. Come back when you do.

sections.title: Delivering the Wise Old Man's Letters

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you come back with at least one inventory space, I'll give you another copy.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Father Aereck in Lumbridge is sure to reward you if you take a note to him for me.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Just head eastwards to Lumbridge. He'll be in the church.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Could you please take a message to the High Priest of Entrana for me?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: There are some monks in Port Sarim, west of here, who will take you there so long as you're not carrying any weapons or armour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Reldo, the librarian in Varrock Palace, wrote to me recently about something. I need you to take my reply to him.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The library is on the ground floor of the Palace in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Thurgo the dwarf is from an ancient tribe. I've found some of its history that he was asking about, and I need you to deliver the information to him.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Go west to Port Sarim, then southwards to some cliffs. He lives on the beach there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I hear my old friend Fr Lawrence in Varrock is drinking a bit too much. I'd like to get a letter to him about it.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Varrock is a fairly long way north-east of here. The church is at the eastern side of the town, near the Palace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've written a letter to my old friend Abbot Langley that I'd like you to deliver.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Walk northwards, past Draynor Manor. When you reach the Barbarian Village, go north-west until you find his Monastery. He'll be downstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'd like to get a note up to the Oracle on Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Here's the letter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Walk northwards, past Draynor Manor. When you reach the Barbarian Village, go north-west until you see the Ice Mountain. It'll be at the top.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, you're meant to be delivering it for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think I need to keep this?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I asked you to deliver that for me. But I may as well take it back now.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You seem to have mislaid my letter, so here's another copy.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please enter the answer to the question.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: How can that be? Try again!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Spot on!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ah! Here you go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I need you to answer this for me.
